plan_name: Digital Spotter — Pi inference pipeline upgrade for v4 BiLSTM+MHA model
generated_from: gym_fusion_model_training_v4.ipynb (executed run: 91.36% test accuracy, F1=0.9257 @ optimal threshold)

new_model_contract.delta_timestep: phase3_features - phase1_features, appended as 4th row in software, computed AFTER all 3 raw phases are buffered
preprocessing_order: winsorize IMU columns (clip to saved bounds)
impute NaN with saved per-feature medians
key_differences_from_old_38: Raw L/R angles (hip/knee/ankle, 6 values) are REMOVED from the feature vector entirely — model never sees them directly, only their |L-R| asymmetry.
Context block now comes FIRST (height, weight, exercise_id, phase, load), not last.
Trunk angle is the ONLY raw angle kept (1 value instead of old 7).
4 brand-new engineered features with no equivalent in the old pipeline: IMU_Acc_Magnitude, Knee_Hip_Coupling_L, Knee_Hip_Coupling_R, Velocity_Decel_Ratio.
2 new subject-context features: Femur_Tibia_Ratio, BMI — both currently entered manually per session per your decision.
Sequence length is 4, not 3 — a delta row computed from phase1/phase3 must be appended before inference.

user_decisions_applied.femur_tibia_ratio_and_bmi: Manual per-session input (like height/weight already are) — collected via /api/session/start, stored on the session row.
user_decisions_applied.imu_hardware: Still mock-only. ESP32Bridge is wired into the live pipeline now (it currently exists but is never instantiated/called anywhere) so realistic mock IMU values flow through the model instead of hardcoded zeros. Swapping to real hardware later is a one-line change in esp32_bridge.py (TODO PHASE_8 already marked).
user_decisions_applied.bar_velocity_and_decel_ratio: Mock IMU bridge already returns v_bar_velocity (see imu/esp32_bridge.py _mock_sample). Velocity_Decel_Ratio is computed from the mock phase1/phase3 bar velocity until real IMU lands — it will not silently be zero/wrong, it will just be mock-quality like the rest of the IMU chain.

files.why: Old constants hardcode the 38-feature / 5-group layout in a different order than the v4 model expects. These are read by feature_engineer.py, sequence_buffer.py (indirectly via config), and tests.

files.why: Built for the old 5-group/38-feature layout in the wrong order with the wrong fields (raw L/R angles instead of asymmetry, no engineered features, no subject context). This is the single most consequential file to get right — a wrong column order silently produces a vector the scaler was never fit on, and the model will make confident-but-wrong predictions with no error thrown.

    # builds 38-feature vector: XY(16) -> angles(7) -> IMU(6) -> bar(3) -> context(6)
changes.new_value:
def engineer(self, landmarks, angles, imu_data=None, context=None, subject=None):
    """Build the complete 40-feature vector matching FEATURE_ORDER.

    Args:
        landmarks: Dict {name: (x, y, z, visibility)}.
        angles: Dict {joint_name: angle_degrees} — needs LEFT/RIGHT_HIP,
            LEFT/RIGHT_KNEE, LEFT/RIGHT_ANKLE, TRUNK.
        imu_data: Optional dict from ESP32Bridge.read_sample() (acc_x..gyro_z,
            v_bar_velocity, p_bar_power, smoothness_jerk). None = zeros.
        context: Optional dict — rep_phase, exercise_id, load_kg.
        subject: Optional dict — height_cm, weight_kg, ftr (manual per-session
            input collected at /api/session/start). None = config defaults.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (40,) float32, ordered per FEATURE_ORDER.
    """
    features = np.zeros(TOTAL_FEATURES, dtype=np.float32)
    idx = 0
    features[idx:idx+5] = self._extract_context(context, subject); idx += 5
    features[idx:idx+16] = self._extract_landmark_xy(landmarks);    idx += 16
    features[idx:idx+1]  = self._extract_trunk_angle(angles);       idx += 1
    features[idx:idx+6]  = self._extract_imu_features(imu_data);    idx += 6
    features[idx:idx+3]  = self._extract_bar_features(imu_data);    idx += 3
    features[idx:idx+3]  = self._compute_asymmetry_features(angles); idx += 3
    features[idx:idx+4]  = self._compute_engineered_features(angles, imu_data); idx += 4
    features[idx:idx+2]  = self._extract_subject_features(subject); idx += 2
    assert idx == TOTAL_FEATURES, f"Feature count mismatch: expected {TOTAL_FEATURES}, got {idx}"
    return features
changes.reason: Matches FEATURE_ORDER group-for-group: context first (5, not 6 — load_kg moved here, height/weight moved here too, FTR/BMI moved to the very end as subject features per the notebook), vision XY (16, unchanged), trunk only (1, not 7), IMU (6, unchanged), bar (3, unchanged), asymmetry (3, NEW — was previously computed but never used), engineered (4, entirely new), subject (2, entirely new).

Returned the 7 raw angles (LEFT_HIP, RIGHT_HIP, LEFT_KNEE, RIGHT_KNEE, LEFT_ANKLE, RIGHT_ANKLE, TRUNK) which the v4 model never sees. Replaced by _extract_trunk_angle() (keeps only TRUNK) and _compute_asymmetry_features() (already existed but was dead code, now actually called).

changes.new_value:
def _extract_trunk_angle(self, angles):
    """Extract the single trunk inclination feature.

    Args:
        angles: Dict of {joint_name: angle_degrees}.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (1,).
    """
    return np.array([angles.get("TRUNK", 0.0)], dtype=np.float32)
changes.reason: v4 model keeps Angle_Trunk_Inclination as the only raw angle; the bilateral joint angles are represented only via asymmetry.
This method already existed and already computes exactly what's needed (hip/knee/ankle |L-R|) — it was just never called from engineer(). No logic change needed, just call it.
Dead code, computes bilateral mean angles which have no slot in the v4 40-feature vector (the model was never trained on this quantity).

changes.new_value:
def _compute_engineered_features(self, angles, imu_data):
    """Compute the 4 v4-only engineered features.

    IMU_Acc_Magnitude: sqrt(ax^2+ay^2+az^2), rotation-invariant movement
        intensity. Gravity baseline ~9.81 when still.
    Knee_Hip_Coupling_L/R: knee_flexion_angle / (hip_flexion_angle + eps).
        Needs the RAW per-side angles, not the asymmetry — read directly
        from `angles` here even though raw angles aren't in the output vector.
    Velocity_Decel_Ratio: placeholder 1.0 (neutral) here — the true value
        needs phase1 AND phase3 bar velocity and is computed once per
        completed rep in SessionManager, then backfilled into the delta
        row before inference. See sequence_buffer.py changes.

    Args:
        angles: Dict of joint angles (raw, includes L/R pairs).
        imu_data: Optional IMU dict, None = zeros/neutral defaults.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (4,) — [IMU_Acc_Magnitude, Knee_Hip_Coupling_L,
            Knee_Hip_Coupling_R, Velocity_Decel_Ratio].
    """
    eps = 1e-6
    if imu_data is None:
        imu_mag = 0.0
    else:
        ax = imu_data.get("acc_x", 0.0)
        ay = imu_data.get("acc_y", 0.0)
        az = imu_data.get("acc_z", 0.0)
        imu_mag = float(np.sqrt(ax**2 + ay**2 + az**2))

    knee_hip_l = angles.get("LEFT_KNEE", 0.0) / (angles.get("LEFT_HIP", 0.0) + eps)
    knee_hip_r = angles.get("RIGHT_KNEE", 0.0) / (angles.get("RIGHT_HIP", 0.0) + eps)

    decel_ratio = 1.0  # neutral placeholder; SessionManager overwrites this
                       # on the delta row once phase1/phase3 velocity are both known

    return np.array([imu_mag, knee_hip_l, knee_hip_r, decel_ratio], dtype=np.float32)
changes.reason: These 4 features did not exist in the old pipeline at all. IMU_Acc_Magnitude and Knee_Hip_Coupling are computable per-frame from data already available (IMU + angles), so they're computed inline here. Velocity_Decel_Ratio is fundamentally cross-phase (needs phase1 AND phase3 readings) so it cannot be correctly computed at single-frame time — a neutral placeholder (1.0, meaning 'no deceleration') is used here and the real value is patched in by SessionManager once the full rep completes (see integration/session_manager.py changes).

changes.new_value:
def _extract_context(self, context, subject):
    """Extract the 5 context features: height, weight, exercise_id, phase, load.

    Args:
        context: Optional dict with rep_phase, exercise_id, load_kg.
        subject: Optional dict with height_cm, weight_kg (manual per-session input).

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (5,) — [height_cm, weight_kg, exercise_id, rep_phase, load_kg].
    """
    context = context or {}
    subject = subject or {}
    return np.array([
        subject.get("height_cm", 175.0),
        subject.get("weight_kg", 75.0),
        context.get("exercise_id", 0),
        context.get("rep_phase", 0),
        context.get("load_kg", 0.0),
    ], dtype=np.float32)
changes.reason: Notebook's FEATURE_ORDER puts Subject_Height_cm, Subject_Weight_kg, Exercise_ID, Rep_Phase, Load_kg first as a group of 5 — different grouping AND different count (5, not the old 6) than the old _extract_context_features, which bundled FTR in here too (FTR now lives in the new _extract_subject_features at the end, matching the notebook's actual final 2 columns).

changes.new_value:
def _extract_subject_features(self, subject):
    """Extract the 2 trailing subject-context features: FTR, BMI.

    Args:
        subject: Optional dict with height_cm, weight_kg, ftr — same dict
            passed to start_session(), manual per-session input.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (2,) — [Femur_Tibia_Ratio, BMI].
            BMI is computed from height_cm/weight_kg if not given directly.
    """
    subject = subject or {}
    height_cm = subject.get("height_cm", 175.0)
    weight_kg = subject.get("weight_kg", 75.0)
    ftr = subject.get("ftr", 1.20)  # ~population average from subject_meta.csv

    bmi = subject.get("bmi")
    if bmi is None:
        height_m = height_cm / 100.0
        bmi = weight_kg / (height_m ** 2) if height_m > 0 else 0.0

    return np.array([ftr, bmi], dtype=np.float32)
changes.reason: Femur_Tibia_Ratio and BMI are the last 2 columns in FEATURE_ORDER, sourced from subject_meta.csv in training. On the Pi there's no subject_meta.csv — per your decision, FTR is entered manually once per session; BMI is auto-computed from height/weight if not explicitly supplied, since you already collect height_cm and weight_kg.

Logic and field names are already correct (acc_x..gyro_z, v_bar_velocity, p_bar_power, smoothness_jerk) and match what ESP32Bridge.read_sample()/_mock_sample() already returns. No change needed beyond being called in the new group order.

Replaced by the split _extract_context() (5 fields, different grouping position) + _extract_subject_features() (2 fields, at the end). The old method's single 6-field bundle doesn't match the v4 layout.

changes.new_value:
def get_model_sequence(self, decel_ratio_idx=None, decel_ratio_value=None):
    """Build the (4, num_features) sequence the model actually expects.

    Appends a 4th row = phase3_features - phase1_features (the delta
    timestep), matching the v4 notebook's augmentation exactly:
        X_delta = X[:, 2, :] - X[:, 0, :]
        X_aug = concatenate([X, X_delta], axis=1)

    Args:
        decel_ratio_idx: Index of Velocity_Decel_Ratio within the feature
            vector (FEATURE_ORDER.index('Velocity_Decel_Ratio')). If given
            along with decel_ratio_value, overwrites the placeholder value
            in the delta row (the only row where this feature carries real
            cross-phase information).
        decel_ratio_value: The real computed decel ratio for this completed
            rep, or None to leave the per-frame placeholder (1.0) in place.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (4, num_features), ready for the scaler/model.
    """
    raw = self._buffer.copy()  # (3, num_features)
    delta = raw[2, :] - raw[0, :]  # phase3 - phase1

    if decel_ratio_idx is not None and decel_ratio_value is not None:
        delta[decel_ratio_idx] = decel_ratio_value

    return np.concatenate([raw, delta[np.newaxis, :]], axis=0)  # (4, num_features)
changes.reason: This is the direct, faithful port of the notebook's delta-augmentation cell (Section 7, 'DELTA-TIMESTEP AUGMENTATION'). Computing it here (rather than in lstm_runner or session_manager) keeps the buffer the single owner of sequence-shape logic, matching the existing class's responsibility.

Still useful as-is for callers who want the raw (3, num_features) buffer (e.g. for DB logging of the 3 captured phases). Renamed conceptually to 'raw sequence' in docstrings only — no behavior change. get_model_sequence() is the new method callers use before inference.

files.why: Only applies StandardScaler. The v4 pipeline requires winsorization (IMU clipping) and median imputation to run BEFORE scaling, in that exact order, or the scaled values will not match what the model was trained on (especially for the 6.4% of reps that had missing sensor readings in training — see notebook Section 8 imputation cell).

Renaming makes the responsibility honest; ScalerWrapper name would be misleading once winsorization/imputation are added. lstm_runner.py import updated accordingly (see that file's entry).

changes.old_value:
def load(self, path=None):
    # loads only scaler.pkl
changes.new_value:
def load(self, scaler_path=None, winsor_path=None, medians_path=None):
    """Load scaler, winsor bounds, and feature medians.

    Each artifact degrades independently and gracefully: if winsor_bounds
    or feature_medians are missing, that step is skipped (passthrough)
    rather than failing — matching the existing fallback philosophy of
    this codebase (mock mode rather than crash).

    Args:
        scaler_path: Path to scaler.save. Uses Config.PATHS.SCALER_PKL.
        winsor_path: Path to winsor_bounds.save. Uses Config.PATHS.WINSOR_BOUNDS.
        medians_path: Path to feature_medians.save. Uses Config.PATHS.FEATURE_MEDIANS.

    Returns:
        bool: True if the scaler (the only load-bearing artifact) loaded.
    """
    import joblib
    scaler_path = Path(scaler_path) if scaler_path else Config.PATHS.SCALER_PKL
    winsor_path = Path(winsor_path) if winsor_path else Config.PATHS.WINSOR_BOUNDS
    medians_path = Path(medians_path) if medians_path else Config.PATHS.FEATURE_MEDIANS

    scaler_ok = self._load_scaler(scaler_path, joblib)
    self._winsor_bounds = self._load_optional_dict(winsor_path, joblib, "winsor bounds")
    self._feature_medians = self._load_optional_dict(medians_path, joblib, "feature medians")
    return scaler_ok
changes.reason: Mirrors the three separate joblib.dump() artifacts the notebook produces (scaler.save, winsor_bounds.save, feature_medians.save). Each is loaded with its own try/except so a missing winsor/median file doesn't take down inference entirely, consistent with how this codebase already treats the model file and scaler as independently optional.

changes.location: New private helper: _load_optional_dict(self, path, joblib_module, label)
changes.new_value:
def _load_optional_dict(self, path, joblib_module, label):
    """Load a joblib dict artifact, returning None on any failure.

    Args:
        path: Path to the .save file.
        joblib_module: Imported joblib module (passed in to avoid
            re-importing per call).
        label: Human-readable name for log messages.

    Returns:
        dict or None.
    """
    if not path.exists():
        logger.warning("%s file not found: %s — skipping this step", label, path)
        return None
    try:
        d = joblib_module.load(str(path))
        logger.info("%s loaded from %s (%d entries)", label, path, len(d))
        return d
    except Exception as e:
        logger.error("Failed to load %s: %s — skipping this step", label, e)
        return None
changes.reason: Shared loader for winsor_bounds and feature_medians, both of which are dicts keyed by feature name -> value (winsor: (lo,hi) tuple; medians: float). Keeps load() readable.

changes.new_value:
def apply_preprocessing(self, sequence):
    """Run the full v4 preprocessing chain: winsorize -> impute -> scale.

    This order is NOT arbitrary — it exactly matches Section 8 of the
    training notebook (winsorization cell runs before the median
    imputation cell, which runs before the StandardScaler fit/transform
    cell). Reversing the order would clip values that imputation should
    have replaced first, or scale before bounds are enforced.

    Args:
        sequence: numpy.ndarray, shape (4, num_features) — the model-ready
            sequence from SequenceBuffer.get_model_sequence().

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (4, num_features), preprocessed and ready
            for the TFLite interpreter (still needs a batch dim added by
            the caller — see lstm_runner.py).
    """
    sequence = np.array(sequence, dtype=np.float32)

    if self._winsor_bounds:
        sequence = self._apply_winsor(sequence)

    if self._feature_medians:
        sequence = self._apply_imputation(sequence)

    return self.transform(sequence)  # existing scaler-only method, unchanged
changes.reason: Single entry point for lstm_runner._preprocess() to call instead of transform() directly, so the runner doesn't need to know about the 3-step chain itself.

changes.new_value:
def _apply_winsor(self, sequence):
    """Clip IMU columns to the saved [lo, hi] bounds per feature.

    Args:
        sequence: numpy.ndarray, shape (timesteps, num_features).

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Same shape, IMU columns clipped.
    """
    from config.constants import FEATURE_ORDER
    out = sequence.copy()
    for fname, (lo, hi) in self._winsor_bounds.items():
        if fname in FEATURE_ORDER:
            fidx = FEATURE_ORDER.index(fname)
            out[:, fidx] = np.clip(out[:, fidx], lo, hi)
    return out

def _apply_imputation(self, sequence):
    """Fill NaN values with the saved per-feature training medians.

    Args:
        sequence: numpy.ndarray, shape (timesteps, num_features).

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Same shape, NaN replaced.
    """
    from config.constants import FEATURE_ORDER
    out = sequence.copy()
    nan_mask = np.isnan(out)
    if not nan_mask.any():
        return out
    for fname, median in self._feature_medians.items():
        if fname in FEATURE_ORDER:
            fidx = FEATURE_ORDER.index(fname)
            col = out[:, fidx]
            col[np.isnan(col)] = median
            out[:, fidx] = col
    return out
changes.reason: Direct ports of the notebook's winsorization and imputation cells (Section 8), but applied to a single live sequence instead of a full train/val/test array. winsor_bounds and feature_medians dicts are keyed by feature name exactly as saved (FEATURE_ORDER entries), so lookups are name-based, not positional, which is more robust to any future column reordering.

changes.new_value:
def _preprocess(self, sequence):
    """Run winsor->impute->scale, then add batch dimension.

    Args:
        sequence: numpy.ndarray of shape (4, 40) — see predict() docstring.

    Returns:
        numpy.ndarray: Shape (1, 4, 40) float32 tensor.
    """
    sequence = np.array(sequence, dtype=np.float32)
    processed = self._preprocessor.apply_preprocessing(sequence)
    return processed.reshape(1, *processed.shape).astype(np.float32)
changes.reason: Calls the new 3-step preprocessing chain instead of scaler-only transform.
Mock prediction logic (random beta distribution) doesn't depend on feature shape, no change needed.

changes.old_value: def start_session(self, exercise_id=0, notes=None):
changes.new_value:
def start_session(self, exercise_id=0, notes=None, height_cm=None,
                   weight_kg=None, ftr=None, load_kg=None):
    """Start a new training session.

    Args:
        exercise_id: Exercise type (0=Squat, 1=Deadlift).
        notes: Optional notes.
        height_cm: Subject height in cm, manual per-session input.
            Defaults to 175.0 if not provided.
        weight_kg: Subject weight in kg, manual per-session input.
            Defaults to 75.0 if not provided.
        ftr: Femur-to-tibia ratio, manual per-session input.
            Defaults to 1.20 (population-average from training data)
            if not provided.
        load_kg: Barbell load for this session, manual per-session input.
            Defaults to 0.0 if not provided.

    Returns:
        int: New session ID.

    Side Effects:
        Creates DB record (including new subject columns), resets
        pipeline, starts processing.
    """
    self._exercise_id = exercise_id
    self._exercise_name = Config.EXERCISES.get_name(exercise_id)

    self._subject = {
        "height_cm": height_cm if height_cm is not None else 175.0,
        "weight_kg": weight_kg if weight_kg is not None else 75.0,
        "ftr": ftr if ftr is not None else 1.20,
    }
    self._current_load_kg = load_kg if load_kg is not None else 0.0

    self._session_id = create_session(
        exercise_id, self._exercise_name, notes,
        height_cm=self._subject["height_cm"],
        weight_kg=self._subject["weight_kg"],
        ftr=self._subject["ftr"],
    )
    self._is_active = True
    self._rep_count = 0
    self._phase1_velocity = None
    self._phase3_velocity = None

    self._frame_processor.reset_session()
    self._sequence_buffer.reset()

    logger.info(
        "Session %d started (exercise=%s, height=%.1fcm, weight=%.1fkg, ftr=%.2f, load=%.1fkg)",
        self._session_id, self._exercise_name,
        self._subject["height_cm"], self._subject["weight_kg"],
        self._subject["ftr"], self._current_load_kg
    )
    return self._session_id
changes.reason: Accepts the manual subject inputs you confirmed (height/weight/FTR per-session, same pattern as the pre-existing height/weight constants) plus load_kg, stores them for use in process_frame_result(), and persists them to the DB via the updated create_session() signature (see database/queries.py entry).

Subject dict (height/weight/ftr) is passed through so FTR/BMI/height/weight features are populated correctly. load_kg now comes from the session's actual manual input instead of a hardcoded 0.0. Bar velocity is captured at phase 1 and phase 3 specifically, since those are the two readings Velocity_Decel_Ratio needs.

changes.new_value:
if not self._sequence_buffer.is_ready():
    return None

# Compute the real Velocity_Decel_Ratio now that phase1 and phase3
# velocities are both known (was a 1.0 placeholder during per-frame
# feature engineering — see feature_engineer._compute_engineered_features)
decel_ratio = 1.0
if self._phase1_velocity is not None and self._phase3_velocity is not None:
    decel_ratio = self._phase3_velocity / (abs(self._phase1_velocity) + 1e-6)

sequence = self._sequence_buffer.get_model_sequence(
    decel_ratio_idx=self._decel_ratio_idx,
    decel_ratio_value=decel_ratio,
)
result = self._lstm_runner.predict(sequence)
self._sequence_buffer.reset()
self._phase1_velocity = None
self._phase3_velocity = None
changes.reason: Replicates the notebook's Velocity_Decel_Ratio formula (Vel_Ph3 / (|Vel_Ph1| + 1e-6), Section 4f) using the real phase1/phase3 bar velocities captured during this rep, patches it into the delta row (the only row where the cross-phase ratio is meaningful), and uses get_model_sequence() (4, 40) instead of get_sequence() (3, 38) so the model receives the shape it was trained on.
No shape or feature dependency, unaffected by this upgrade.

changes.new_value:
def create_session(exercise_id, exercise_name, notes=None,
                    height_cm=175.0, weight_kg=75.0, ftr=1.20):
    """Create a new training session.

    Args:
        exercise_id: Numeric exercise identifier (0=Squat, 1=Deadlift).
        exercise_name: Human-readable exercise name string.
        notes: Optional user notes for the session.
        height_cm: Subject height in cm, manual per-session input.
        weight_kg: Subject weight in kg, manual per-session input.
        ftr: Femur-to-tibia ratio, manual per-session input.

    Returns:
        int: The newly created session ID.
    """
    conn = get_connection()
    cursor = conn.execute(
        """INSERT INTO sessions
               (exercise_id, exercise_name, notes, height_cm, weight_kg, ftr)
           VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
        (exercise_id, exercise_name, notes, height_cm, weight_kg, ftr)
    )
    conn.commit()
    session_id = cursor.lastrowid
    logger.info(
        "Created session %d for %s (h=%.1fcm w=%.1fkg ftr=%.2f)",
        session_id, exercise_name, height_cm, weight_kg, ftr
    )
    return session_id
changes.reason: Matches the new schema columns and the new SessionManager.start_session() call signature.

files.known_pre_existing_gap_not_introduced_by: api/routes.py keeps its own module-level `_lstm_runner = LSTMRunner()` instance, completely separate from the one inside integration/session_manager.py's SessionManager. This means /api/session/start's create_session() call does NOT currently flow into the live SessionManager's start_session() (which is what actually needs the subject dict for inference). This disconnect predates this upgrade. Recommended follow-up (not included in this plan's file changes since it's an architectural decision, not a v4-compatibility fix): give api/routes.py a reference to the live SessionManager instance (same pattern as set_frame_processor()) and call session_mgr.start_session(...) from this route instead of create_session() directly.

| `model_fullprec.tflite` | Full precision fallback | Section 13 of training notebook |
| `scaler.save` | StandardScaler fit on (4,40) sequences | Section 8 of training notebook |
| `winsor_bounds.save` | dict[feature_name -> (lo, hi)] for IMU clipping | Section 8 of training notebook |
| `feature_medians.save` | dict[feature_name -> median] for NaN imputation | Section 8 of training notebook |
| `deployment_config.save` | dict with 'threshold' (0.359), 'feature_cols', 'window_size_model' | Section 13 of training notebook |
changes.reason: Reflects every artifact the notebook now produces and that the upgraded inference pipeline loads. deployment_config.save isn't strictly required by the code changes above (threshold is hardcoded into config.py as 0.359 per this plan), but listing it documents where that number came from and gives an alternative load path if you'd rather read the threshold dynamically than hardcode it.

changes.new_value:
- Model expects exactly 40 features in the order defined by `config.constants.FEATURE_ORDER` and a 4-timestep sequence (3 real phases + 1 computed delta row). See `inference/sequence_buffer.py:get_model_sequence()`.
- winsor_bounds.save and feature_medians.save are optional at runtime (inference degrades gracefully without them, same fallback philosophy as the model/scaler) but predictions will not match training-time behavior without them, especially for sessions with occluded/missing sensor frames.
changes.reason: Documents the breaking shape/order change for whoever deploys model files to the Pi.

assert asym.shape == (3,)

assert asym[1] == abs(angles["LEFT_KNEE"] - angles["RIGHT_KNEE"])
assert asym[2] == abs(angles["LEFT_ANKLE"] - angles["RIGHT_ANKLE"])

def test_asymmetry_is_actually_used_in_output_vector(self):
    """Was dead code before this upgrade — now must appear at indices 31-33."""
    fe = FeatureEngineer()
    angles = self._make_angles()
    features = fe.engineer(self._make_landmarks(), angles)
    expected_hip_asym = abs(angles["LEFT_HIP"] - angles["RIGHT_HIP"])
    assert abs(features[31] - expected_hip_asym) < 1e-5

files.why: Should record this as a breaking change for anyone else touching the repo.

changes.new_value:
## [Unreleased] — v4 model compatibility upgrade
### BREAKING
- Feature vector grew from 38 to 40 features; column order changed completely
  (raw L/R angles removed, replaced by asymmetry + 4 new engineered features +
  2 new subject features). Old model_quantized.tflite / scaler.pkl are NOT
  compatible with the upgraded inference code.
- Sequence length grew from 3 to 4 timesteps (added computed delta row).
- scaler.pkl renamed to scaler.save; two new required-for-correctness
  artifacts added: winsor_bounds.save, feature_medians.save.
- Decision threshold changed from 0.5 to 0.359 (notebook-optimized).
- sessions table gained height_cm, weight_kg, ftr columns — run
  scripts/migrate_v4_schema.py against any existing data/sessions.db.
### Added
- ESP32Bridge now wired into the live inference loop (mock mode).
- Manual per-session subject input (height/weight/FTR) via /api/session/start.
### Fixed
- _compute_asymmetry_features() was dead code, never called from
  FeatureEngineer.engineer() — now actually used.
changes.reason: Standard changelog hygiene for a breaking change of this size.

deployment_checklist: 1. Copy model_quantized.tflite, model_fullprec.tflite, scaler.save, winsor_bounds.save, feature_medians.save from the executed v4 notebook's saved_models/ into models/ on the Pi.
2. Apply all file changes listed above.
3. Run scripts/migrate_v4_schema.py once against the existing data/sessions.db.
4. Run scripts/validate_model.py — should report input shape (1, 4, 40).
5. Run python -m pytest tests/test_03_inference/ -v — confirm updated shape assertions pass.

6. Manually smoke-test one full session via the API: POST /api/session/start with height_cm/weight_kg/ftr/load_kg, run a rep through the vision pipeline, confirm /api/session/<id> shows a real (non-mock) inference_label.
7. Check logs/app.log for 'LSTM model loaded' and 'Scaler loaded' (not the mock-mode warnings) to confirm real artifacts were picked up, not silently falling back to mock.
